planning_rules: Keep runtime-schema parsing separate from solver semantics.
Keep state-model changes separate from kernel or numerics changes.
Use milestone stop points as PR boundaries whenever the phase defines them.
Do not combine the first correctness implementation with the first optimization implementation.
Land validation/reporting harnesses before the baseline-freeze PR of a phase.

Do not let one PR straddle two numerically risky couplings at once, especially alpha+pressure, pressure hook+BC semantics, graph capture+backend replacement, or segmented kernels+wetting/contact-angle scope.
recommended_top_level_execution_order: Phase 0 and Phase 1 next; they can overlap once the authority and pin ingestion exist, but Phase 0 sign-off must complete before any correctness-sensitive GPU comparisons.
Phase 2 must be green before Phase 3.
Phase 4 can start after Phase 1/2 are stable enough to support the pressure bridge and snapshot tooling.
Phase 5 starts only after Phase 2, Phase 3, and the Phase 4 native pressure path are available.
Phase 6 starts only after the Phase 5 generic VOF core is frozen.
Phase 7 starts only after Phase 6 nozzle semantics are stable and hotspot data exists.
Phase 8 instrumentation PRs should start early, but formal baseline locks wait until the Phase 7 path is stable.

prs.title: Authority ingestion scaffold
prs.scope: Add a shared loader/validator for the package authority docs and JSON companions so later phases consume typed program-wide decisions instead of hard-coded constants.
prs.done_when: One command/library path loads the authority set, validates schema/versioning, and exposes a stable API used by downstream tools.

prs.title: Pin-manifest consumption and environment manifest emission
prs.scope: Implement ingestion of the master pin manifest and emit machine-readable environment manifests for builds, runs, and profiling artifacts.
prs.done_when: Build/run tooling can resolve the frozen toolchain lane and emit `host_env` / manifest artifacts without duplicating pin logic.

prs.title: Reference-case and validation-ladder utilities
prs.scope: Create helpers that resolve canonical case IDs, ladder roles (`R2`, `R1-core`, `R1`, `R0`), and phase-gate selection.
prs.done_when: Scripts and tests can select cases by ladder role and reject unknown or out-of-scope case IDs.

prs.title: Support-matrix scanner and fail-fast policy
prs.scope: Implement centralized support scanning for BCs, schemes, turbulence scope, function objects, backend eligibility, and fallback policy.
prs.done_when: Unsupported tuples are rejected before the run starts, and debug-only fallback stays non-default and explicit.

prs.title: Acceptance-manifest evaluator scaffold
prs.scope: Build a reusable acceptance evaluator that understands tuple IDs, tolerance classes, parity classes, hard/soft gates, and waiver hooks.
prs.done_when: Later phases can submit artifacts to one common evaluator and get deterministic pass/fail dispositions tied to manifest entries.

prs.title: Graph stage registry and graph-support-matrix loader
prs.scope: Create the canonical stage-ID registry and graph-policy loader used by execution, instrumentation, and acceptance code.
prs.done_when: All graph-aware code resolves stage IDs from one shared source, and tuple-to-stage validation runs without local restatement.

prs.title: Semantic source-audit helper
prs.scope: Add tooling and templates that map semantic source references to the exact local SPUMA/v2412 patch targets before any solver modifications.
prs.done_when: Each implementation phase can generate a reviewed source-audit note and avoid patching the wrong upstream analog.

prs.title: Environment probe hardening
prs.scope: Extend the existing OpenFOAM environment probe so both Baseline A and Baseline B resolve into machine-readable manifests, with no hard-coded `/opt/openfoam12` assumptions left in tooling.
prs.done_when: The probe captures environment details for both baselines and generated tooling no longer embeds fixed OpenFOAM paths.

prs.title: Environment-neutral runner wrappers
prs.scope: Add runner wrappers that source the probed environment explicitly and execute the staged workflow in a baseline-neutral way.
prs.done_when: Generated case runners execute against both baselines without manual script edits or hidden environment coupling.

prs.title: Case metadata and stage-plan emission
prs.scope: Extend the builder/orchestrator to emit `stage_plan.json`, richer `case_meta.json`, resolved numerics, startup-seed metadata, and provenance fields needed by later comparisons.
prs.done_when: Every generated case carries a complete machine-readable execution plan and resolved metadata bundle.

prs.title: I/O normalization overlay
prs.scope: Implement the non-physics output normalization layer for ASCII/uncompressed/fixed-precision output so comparison artifacts are stable across reruns.
prs.done_when: Same-baseline reruns produce stable normalized outputs and do not fail due to incidental I/O formatting drift.

prs.title: Fingerprints, field signatures, and extractor JSON
prs.scope: Add mesh/patch fingerprints, selected field-signature extraction, and JSON output from the feature extractor for automated comparison workflows.
prs.done_when: R2/R1-core/R1/R0 cases can emit comparison-ready fingerprints and field signatures non-interactively.

prs.title: Baseline A reference freeze
prs.scope: Generate and freeze the Baseline A reference bundles for `R2`, `R1-core`, `R1`, and `R0`, including artifacts, metadata, and initial tolerance checks.
prs.done_when: A complete Baseline A bundle exists for all ladder cases and becomes the initial control truth.

prs.title: Baseline B bring-up and R2 smoke
prs.scope: Bring up Baseline B in build-only mode first, validate that no legacy OpenFOAM-12 coupling remains, then run the `R2` smoke case with documented backend policy.
prs.done_when: Baseline B builds the frozen cases and `R2` executes cleanly enough to justify proceeding to nozzle cases.

prs.title: Baseline B nozzle freeze and sign-off package
prs.scope: Run/freeze `R1` and `R0` on Baseline B, compare against Baseline A, and generate the Phase 0 sign-off packet with compare JSON/Markdown and archived artifacts.
prs.done_when: Phase 0 ends with reviewed Baseline A/B compare reports and a frozen reference contract suitable for downstream implementation.

prs.title: Host and CUDA discovery probes
prs.scope: Add the Phase 1 host/CUDA discovery binaries and emit `host_env.json` and `cuda_probe.json` for the target workstation lane.
prs.done_when: The workstation characteristics, driver state, compute capability, and CUDA availability are captured in reproducible machine-readable form.

prs.title: Blackwell build-system enablement
prs.scope: Patch or wrap the build so the primary lane emits `sm_120` plus PTX, adopts NVTX3, and respects the frozen toolchain lane.
prs.done_when: A clean primary-lane build is produced with the required Blackwell targets and without relying on ad hoc local edits.

prs.title: Fatbinary inspection and reporting
prs.scope: Add binary inspection tooling that verifies the expected native cubin/PTX composition and writes `fatbinary_report.json`.
prs.done_when: The build artifact proves `sm_120` coverage and PTX retention before smoke cases begin.

prs.title: Repo-local smoke-case pack and solver audit
prs.scope: Prepare the repo-local smoke cases and the `fvSolution`/runtime sanity checks that gate Phase 1 bring-up.
prs.done_when: All required smoke cases run unprofiled, and unsupported solver settings are caught before deeper tooling passes.

prs.title: Compute Sanitizer memcheck lane
prs.scope: Add the reduced memcheck workflow for the smallest bring-up case so memory errors are caught before profiling work proceeds.
prs.done_when: The smallest supported case passes memcheck with no actionable errors.

prs.title: Nsight Systems baseline and UVM diagnostic traces
prs.scope: Create the basic Nsight Systems profiling lane, the UVM-focused diagnostic lane, and the initial NVTX smoke instrumentation used in Phase 1.
prs.done_when: Both baseline and UVM traces show GPU kernels and visible NVTX ranges, and recurring unexplained UVM migration is eliminated or documented.

prs.title: PTX-JIT proof and Phase 1 acceptance bundle
prs.scope: Run the PTX-JIT compatibility proof and generate the Phase 1 acceptance reports that freeze the bring-up result.
prs.done_when: PTX-JIT succeeds on the workstation lane and `phase1_acceptance_report.json`, `phase1_acceptance_report.md`, and `phase1_acceptance_bundle_index.json` are complete and reviewable.

prs.title: Canonical `gpuRuntime.memory` parser
prs.scope: Implement the authoritative memory-configuration parser, enums, and compatibility-shim handling for the Phase 2 contract.
prs.done_when: The branch has one normalized memory config tree and all later memory code resolves policy from it.

prs.title: Device persistent pool
prs.scope: Add the persistent device allocator for long-lived hot objects and the unit tests that verify deterministic allocation behavior.
prs.done_when: Persistent allocations are pooled, leak-free, and validated independently of solver integration.

prs.title: Device scratch pool
prs.scope: Implement the transient device scratch allocator for timestep-local workspaces with reset/reuse semantics.
prs.done_when: Scratch allocation/reuse works across repeated iterations without unexpected growth or churn.

prs.title: Pinned stage allocator
prs.scope: Add the pooled `cudaMallocHost`-backed stage allocator for explicit write/restart/output staging.
prs.done_when: Field-scale host staging goes through one explicit pinned path and unit tests cover allocation/reuse behavior.

prs.title: Managed fallback allocator
prs.scope: Implement the managed-memory fallback allocator and its policy wiring as a correctness/debug lane rather than a production default.
prs.done_when: Managed fallback exists for controlled bring-up but is clearly segregated from production-device acceptance.

prs.title: Residency registry and reporting
prs.scope: Create the residency state machine, registry types, and reporting layer for all registered hot objects.
prs.done_when: Registered objects have explicit host/device visibility state and the branch can emit deterministic residency reports.

prs.title: Mirror traits and field mirrors
prs.scope: Implement `MirrorTraits` and `FieldMirror` support for OpenFOAM field/list types, including round-trip tests.
prs.done_when: Core field abstractions can be mirrored between host and device with tested round-trip correctness.

prs.title: Mesh mirror and startup registration
prs.scope: Add `MeshMirror` support plus startup registration of mesh topology, addressing, and persistent fields into the registry/pools.
prs.done_when: A supported GPU solver can complete startup upload of the hot mesh/field set using the Phase 2 substrate.

prs.title: Explicit visibility APIs and output stager
prs.scope: Implement `ensureHostVisible`, `ensureDeviceVisible`, scalar staging, and the `OutputStager` so write/restart boundaries are explicit.
prs.done_when: All field-scale host visibility in supported paths goes through explicit APIs and write-boundary correctness tests pass.

prs.title: Compute-epoch enforcement
prs.scope: Add `CpuTouchGuard`, per-epoch logging, and strict mode so illegal CPU touches are detected inside the compute epoch.
prs.done_when: Strict mode catches unsupported CPU access in the supported GPU solver path before Phase 3 begins.

prs.title: Scratch catalog and Phase 2 gate bundle
prs.scope: Implement `ScratchCatalog`, named scratch reset/watermark tracking, and the Phase 2 benchmark/acceptance bundle.
prs.done_when: Repeated iterations show stable scratch watermarks and the Phase 2 gate proves no uncontrolled UVM traffic for registered hot objects.

prs.title: Synchronization and stream inventory
prs.scope: Inventory existing sync points, define explicit stream ownership, and lock down the initial stream policy.
prs.done_when: The branch has a reviewed sync/stream inventory and no hidden stream ownership in supported paths.

prs.title: Execution-mode parser and selection policy
prs.scope: Implement runtime parsing and selection for the supported execution modes (including graph-disabled and graph-enabled lanes).
prs.done_when: Execution mode is selected deterministically from the normalized runtime tree with clear downgrade behavior.

prs.title: GpuExecutionContext and execution registry
prs.scope: Add the central `GpuExecutionContext` and registry that own stream handles, mode selection, and stage execution state.
prs.done_when: All supported GPU stages resolve execution context through one owner rather than ad hoc local state.

prs.title: Async launch wrapper layer
prs.scope: Introduce graph-ready async launch wrappers and remove implicit hot-path device-wide synchronization from the wrapped stages.
prs.done_when: Wrapped stages launch through a consistent async interface and no longer rely on hidden `cudaDeviceSynchronize()` behavior.

prs.title: Canonical stage scaffolding and parent NVTX ranges
prs.scope: Add the canonical stage boundaries and parent-stage NVTX scaffolding imported from the graph support matrix.
prs.done_when: Stage IDs are stable, shared, and visible to instrumentation before the first captured stage lands.

prs.title: First graph-enabled stage
prs.scope: Capture and replay the first approved graph-enabled stage using the Phase 3 execution contract.
prs.done_when: At least one allowed stage captures and replays correctly with stable addresses and no illegal capture-time behavior.

prs.title: Graph fingerprint, cache, and rebuild policy
prs.scope: Implement graph fingerprinting, cache lookup, update/rebuild policy, and parameter mirror handling for capture reuse.
prs.done_when: Steady-state runs reuse captured graphs when valid and rebuild only under documented change conditions.

prs.title: Write-boundary staging, production residency assertions, and Phase 3 acceptance
prs.scope: Integrate explicit write-boundary staging with execution modes, add production residency assertions, and generate the Phase 3 acceptance packet.
prs.done_when: Supported execution modes pass acceptance with write paths outside the timed/captured steady-state window and no hot-path global sync regressions.

prs.title: Dependency freeze and standalone AmgX smoke
prs.scope: Pin the SPUMA / foamExternalSolvers / AmgX dependency line for Phase 4 and build the standalone AmgX smoke test on the target workstation.
prs.done_when: An identity or trivial system solves correctly on the RTX 5080 before OpenFOAM integration begins.

prs.title: Pressure snapshot dump utility
prs.scope: Add the CPU/reference-side pressure snapshot utility so representative matrices, RHS vectors, and metadata can be captured from frozen cases.
prs.done_when: Reduced reference cases can emit reusable pressure snapshots for offline replay and validation.

prs.title: Topology-only LDU-to-CSR builder
prs.scope: Implement the topology conversion that builds CSR row offsets and column indices from the OpenFOAM/SPUMA LDU structure and caches it by topology.
prs.done_when: Synthetic and snapshot-backed tests prove the structural CSR mapping is correct and reusable.

prs.title: CSR value packer and `A*x` validator
prs.scope: Add the value packer that fills CSR coefficients/RHS from live or snapshot data and validate the mapping with `A*x` equivalence checks.
prs.done_when: Snapshot and synthetic `A*x` checks pass within tolerance, separating mapping bugs from solver-backend bugs.

prs.title: AmgX context wrapper and replay utility
prs.scope: Implement the `AmgXContext` wrapper plus a replay tool that loads snapshots and exercises upload/setup/solve/update in isolation.
prs.done_when: Snapshot replay solves correctly and emits timing decomposition without involving the live pressure loop.

prs.title: PressureMatrixCache and persistent staging buffers
prs.scope: Add the persistent pressure-matrix cache object plus reusable host staging buffers that outlive individual solver objects.
prs.done_when: Repeated solves with unchanged topology reuse the cached structure and avoid rebuilding row/column data.

prs.title: Runtime-selected live solver integration
prs.scope: Wire native and AmgX pressure solver selection into the live solver path with clean fallback on unsupported interface conditions.
prs.done_when: A reduced live case can select AmgX at runtime or fall back cleanly to native without corrupting the pressure path.

prs.title: Telemetry, profiling hooks, and reduced-case validation
prs.scope: Add pressure telemetry, NVTX boundaries, native-vs-AmgX benchmark scripts, and reduced-case validation for the Phase 4 live path.
prs.done_when: Phase 4 can prove no repeated topology upload, can compare native vs AmgX on the reduced case, and emits reviewable telemetry artifacts.

prs.title: DeviceDirect pressure bridge
prs.scope: Implement the device-resident `csrValuesDev` / `rhsDev` / `xDev` bridge and the device-pointer upload/replace path required for later no-field-scale-host-transfer claims.
prs.done_when: Replay and reduced live cases validate the device-pointer path, while `PinnedHost` remains correctness-only and clearly non-production.

prs.title: Phase 5 symbol reconciliation note
prs.scope: Generate and review the exact mapping from semantic sources to the local SPUMA/v2412 files touched by the generic VOF port.
prs.done_when: The implementation branch has a reviewed patch-target note before any Phase 5 solver edits land.

prs.title: VOF runtime gate and support scan
prs.scope: Implement the `gpuRuntime.vof` / compatibility-shim gate plus deterministic support scanning for the allowed Phase 5 envelope.
prs.done_when: Unsupported generic VOF tuples fail fast before the timestep loop and accepted tuples are explicitly identified.

prs.title: Persistent topology and boundary-map substrate
prs.scope: Build the Phase 5 topology, cell/face connectivity, and patch descriptor substrate needed by the generic VOF device path.
prs.done_when: The generic VOF path has stable device topology/boundary metadata and emits a memory report for the accepted reduced cases.

prs.title: Field mirrors and old-time state for VOF
prs.scope: Mirror the Phase 5 field set, including old-time/previous-correction state needed by alpha transport and pressure coupling.
prs.done_when: Required VOF fields are device-visible with explicit host authority boundaries and old-time state is preserved correctly.

prs.title: Alpha skeleton path
prs.scope: Implement the end-to-end alpha skeleton path with control snapshots, alpha-flux formation, and placeholder/predictor-only logic.
prs.done_when: The alpha stage runs end-to-end on the reduced case and establishes the device control-flow skeleton without claiming bounded correctness yet.

prs.title: Full alpha + MULES + subcycling
prs.scope: Complete the bounded alpha path, explicit MULES limiter logic, previous-correction flux behavior, and alpha subcycling semantics.
prs.done_when: Reduced generic VOF cases keep alpha bounded and numerically consistent with the frozen CPU reference under the accepted scheme subset.

prs.title: Mixture update and interface/surface-tension subset
prs.scope: Implement `rho` / `rhoPhi` updates plus the constant-sigma interface normals/curvature/surface-tension slice admitted in Phase 5 scope.
prs.done_when: Mixture properties and the restricted interface path validate on the designated reduced tests without reopening contact-angle scope.

prs.title: Momentum predictor
prs.scope: Add the device momentum-predictor path, including persistent `rAU` handling and the accepted laminar reduced-case wiring.
prs.done_when: The reduced laminar case exercises the device `U` equation path successfully and preserves the expected predictor semantics.

prs.title: Native pressure backend integration
prs.scope: Integrate the native pressure corrector path, including non-orthogonal loops, into the device-authoritative Phase 5 solver.
prs.done_when: A reduced nozzle-friendly case completes the native pressure corrector path correctly with the Phase 5 generic core.

prs.title: AmgX pressure backend integration
prs.scope: Wire the runtime-selectable AmgX backend into the Phase 5 solver behind the DeviceDirect gate and compare it against native behavior.
prs.done_when: Accepted reduced tuples can use the AmgX path without violating the no-field-scale-host-transfer contract.

prs.title: Write-time commit, validation artifacts, and Phase 5 baseline freeze
prs.scope: Implement the explicit write-time commit path, restart/reload parity checks, validation/profile artifact generation, and the final Phase 5 baseline freeze.
prs.done_when: Phase 5 ends with a reviewable reduced-case baseline, restart/reload parity evidence, and artifacts ready for Phase 6 handoff.

sections.section: Phase 6 — Pressure-swirl nozzle boundary conditions and startup

prs.title: Boundary support report and patch classifier
prs.scope: Implement the boundary support report, patch classifier, and manifest substrate for the frozen nozzle patch set.
prs.done_when: The branch can classify the R1/R0 boundary set up front and reject unsupported nozzle configurations deterministically.

prs.title: Flat boundary spans and boundary metadata upload
prs.scope: Add the flat boundary-span representation and uploadable metadata views required by device-side boundary execution.
prs.done_when: Supported nozzle cases expose flat boundary spans and patch metadata without falling back to host patch polymorphism.

prs.title: Constrained profile grammar and compiler
prs.scope: Implement the constrained inlet-profile grammar/parser/compiler used by the frozen nozzle boundary contract.
prs.done_when: Accepted inlet profiles compile into a machine-readable representation that the CPU reference and device path both understand.

prs.title: Custom `gpuPressureSwirlInletVelocity` type and CPU snapshot path
prs.scope: Add the custom nozzle inlet runtime type plumbing together with the CPU snapshot/reference path used to validate inlet semantics before device execution.
prs.done_when: The custom inlet BC can be instantiated, and CPU-side snapshot tests prove the frozen inlet math before GPU kernels land.

prs.title: Alpha boundary kernels
prs.scope: Implement the accepted alpha BC kernels (`fixedValue`, `zeroGradient`, `inletOutlet`) for the nozzle envelope.
prs.done_when: Frozen-field boundary tests pass for the accepted alpha patch kinds without host patch evaluation in the hot loop.

prs.title: Ambient/open velocity boundary kernels
prs.scope: Implement the standard ambient/open velocity kernels admitted by the support matrix for nozzle cases.
prs.done_when: The accepted open-boundary velocity subset runs correctly on device and passes frozen snapshot tests.

prs.title: Swirl inlet device kernel and invariance tests
prs.scope: Implement the custom swirl inlet device kernel, including basis regularization and integrated-flux invariance checks.
prs.done_when: Synthetic annular-patch tests and inlet-flux invariance checks pass before full solver integration.

prs.title: Pressure boundary integration
prs.scope: Implement the accepted `prgh*` pressure kernels plus the `fixedFluxPressure` gradient update and the associated pressure-assembly hook as one coupled change.
prs.done_when: `snGradp` matches the CPU reference on frozen tests and a short R1 transient passes the numerically risky pressure/BC coupling point.

prs.title: Startup seeding subsystem
prs.scope: Implement the canonical startup-seed DSL/parser, the seed-region kernel, and the post-seed refresh sequence used by the accepted nozzle path.
prs.done_when: Seed masks and initialized fields match the frozen reference exactly on the accepted startup cases.

prs.title: Solver-stage integration, graph-safety hardening, and Phase 6 acceptance
prs.scope: Wire the nozzle boundary and startup subsystem into alpha/momentum/pressure stages, add runtime-parameter update handling, remove transient allocations, and run the Phase 6 acceptance pass.
prs.done_when: Full R1 runs complete with device-side nozzle BC/startup behavior, graph-safety audits are clean, and the R0 regression package is ready for review.

prs.title: Phase 7 source audit and hotspot ranking
prs.scope: Generate the reviewed Phase 7 source-audit note and produce the hotspot ranking artifact that bounds custom-kernel scope.
prs.done_when: Custom-kernel work is explicitly limited to support-matrix-approved hotspot families ranked from real profiling data.

prs.title: Control plane, POD views, and façade skeleton
prs.scope: Implement the Phase 7 controls parser, POD device views, and the host-side façade that hides kernel-launch details from solver code.
prs.done_when: The custom-kernel subsystem has a narrow, typed interface and runtime control surface before any optimized kernel work lands.

prs.title: Adjacency preprocessing
prs.scope: Build the adjacency preprocessing needed by segmented/gather execution, keeping it separate from kernel correctness work.
prs.done_when: Adjacency structures are generated once, validated, and reusable by both the atomic and segmented backends.

prs.title: Persistent scratch arena
prs.scope: Implement the persistent scratch arena and its ownership/lifetime rules for custom-kernel hot paths.
prs.done_when: Custom kernels can acquire named scratch resources without allocation churn, and watermarks remain stable across repeated iterations.

prs.title: Atomic alpha/MULES correctness backend
prs.scope: Implement the atomic baseline for the Phase 7 alpha/MULES hotspot family as the first correctness line.
prs.done_when: The atomic backend is numerically correct on the accepted reduced/nozzle cases and benchmark artifacts are captured at the milestone stop.

prs.title: Interface and patch kernels
prs.scope: Implement the accepted interface and patch kernel family for the exact patch/scheme subset used by `R1` / `R0`.
prs.done_when: The supported interface/patch kernels pass unit tests and integrate without reopening contact-angle scope.

prs.title: Segmented/gather production backend
prs.scope: Implement the segmented/gather production backend and compare it against the atomic path on the profiled hotspot set.
prs.done_when: A production backend exists that improves or otherwise justifies replacing the atomic baseline for accepted tuples.

prs.title: Graph-safety cleanup, capture validation, and final regression package
prs.scope: Remove remaining capture hazards, validate graph compatibility of the chosen custom-kernel path, and generate the final Phase 7 regression/comparison package.
prs.done_when: The selected custom-kernel path is capture-safe for the approved stages, UVM-clean in steady state, and backed by final regression artifacts.

prs.title: NVTX wrapper library and build flags
prs.scope: Add the permanent NVTX3 wrapper library, compile-time switches, and dependency wiring required by the profiling subsystem.
prs.done_when: Profiling instrumentation can be compiled in/out cleanly and is available to all supported solver modules.

prs.title: Canonical profiling and acceptance config parser
prs.scope: Implement the normalized `gpuRuntime.profiling` / `gpuRuntime.acceptance` parser and compatibility shims.
prs.done_when: All profiling and acceptance behavior is runtime-configurable from the canonical tree with no local shadow schema.

prs.title: Solver-stage instrumentation coverage
prs.scope: Instrument the top-level timestep/PIMPLE/alpha/momentum/pressure/surface/boundary/output stages with the canonical NVTX naming hierarchy.
prs.done_when: The required solver-stage ranges appear in traces for accepted tuples and are aligned with the canonical stage registry.

prs.title: Graph lifecycle instrumentation
prs.scope: Instrument graph capture/build/replay/update lifecycle events so graph behavior is visible in traces and parsers.
prs.done_when: Graph-enabled runs emit the required graph lifecycle markers without changing solver semantics.

prs.title: Nsight Systems capture scripts and artifact layout
prs.scope: Create the Phase 8 `nsys` capture scripts, mode selection, environment manifests, and artifact directory layout.
prs.done_when: A baseline `R1` timeline can be captured end-to-end in the standardized artifact layout with reproducible command lines.

prs.title: Stats export and profile-acceptance parser
prs.scope: Implement report export from `nsys` outputs and the acceptance parser that maps metrics back to manifest-driven hard/soft gates.
prs.done_when: The branch can produce a first formal `R1` acceptance summary from captured profiling artifacts.

prs.title: Diagnostic profiling modes
prs.scope: Add the `uvmAudit`, `syncAudit`, and `graphDebug` modes, keeping them diagnostically useful but outside baseline production timing mode.
prs.done_when: Reduced `R1` runs can separately audit unexpected UVM traffic, inner-loop synchronization, and graph structure with stable artifacts.

prs.title: Top-kernel NCU and sanitizer automation
prs.scope: Add the top-five-kernel Nsight Compute wrapper, section configuration, Compute Sanitizer wrapper, and nightly reduced-case automation.
prs.done_when: The project can produce top-kernel reports and clean reduced-case sanitizer logs without contaminating baseline timing runs.

prs.title: Baseline locks and CI/nightly integration
prs.scope: Run the formal `R1` / `R0` acceptance framework, lock the first approved baselines, and integrate the scheduled profiling/sanitizer jobs.
prs.done_when: The first approved `R0` acceptance bundle exists, `R1` nightly / `R0` scheduled jobs are wired, and retention/lock policy is operational.

final_sequencing_notes: Treat each PR above as the smallest recommended merge unit. If implementation reality forces a split, split downward; do not merge adjacent PRs upward across risky boundaries.
For any PR that introduces a new runtime key, loader, or artifact schema, the parser/schema work should land before solver integration work that consumes it.
For any PR that introduces a new numerically active stage, add its unit/snapshot/reduced-case validation in the same PR or in the immediately following PR before moving to the next coupling point.
The final PR of each phase should be a freeze/acceptance PR, not the same PR that first lands the underlying capability.
